An existing model judges both the pretrained and the finetuned checkpoint on in-distribution completions and in-world instructions, and runs a demoted real-world knowledge probe. A future redesign would add in-context binding as the primary test.

Stage five. run_all evaluates the pretrain checkpoint and, if present, the sft checkpoint separately, writing report_pretrain and report_sft. Each judges in-distribution completions for grammar, coherence, and referent-freedom, and in-world instructions for coherence and instruction-following; a real-world knowledge probe is kept but labeled unreliable at this scale.

Evaluation uses an existing model as judge. It was reworked from judging only the SFT model on out-of-distribution real-world questions (which produced systematically misleading gibberish-looking scores) to judging the base pretrained model on in-distribution completions and in-world instructions, which is the reliable signal at small scale; the SFT checkpoint is now also evaluated separately when present, so the effect of finetuning is directly visible in the two reports (see node 30 for what that comparison has shown). The knowledge probe is kept but explicitly demoted since a tiny model answers out-of-distribution factual questions poorly regardless of referent-freedom. The processor-not-oracle reframing (node 32) argues this probe should eventually be joined, and possibly outranked, by in-context binding tasks (node 33), which are not yet implemented.
